observation: I can see the Specter.AI trading platform homepage with a prominent 'Trade smarter with AI-powered insights' headline. There are two main action buttons visible: 'Create Account' and 'Get Started'. As an elderly user looking to sign up, I can see the 'Create Account' button which is what I need to click to begin the registration process. The button appears to be reasonably sized and visible.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user with limited tech experience, I need to start the sign-up process. The 'Create Account' button is clearly labeled and appears to be the correct option for beginning registration. This is more direct than the 'Get Started' button which might just be informational.
ux_issues: The text on the page could be larger for elderly users - the main headline and description text appears small

outcome.visual_observation: UX issues detected: The text on the page could be larger for elderly users - the main headline and description text appears small
outcome.diagnosis: Analysis completed - see evidence for details

issues: The text on the page could be larger for elderly users - the main headline and description text appears small

dual_diagnosis.visual_observation: The page shows only a red loading circle/spinner in the center with no visible progress in the sign-up flow. The original content (heading, buttons, analytics section) has disappeared but no new sign-up form or next step is visible.
dual_diagnosis.diagnosis: The Create Account button tap appears to have triggered a loading state but failed to complete the navigation to the actual sign-up form. This could be due to network issues, backend errors, or a broken route.
